the startup was bought by another startup and then a bunch of us started a company together that was my
first time as a ceo and then that led to probably the most formative job i've had in my career so far
and it's when i was the founding ceo of a company called automatic which was the company behind wordpress.com
and i remember early wordpress days this is now 20 years ago um a room not dissimilar to this a few
hundred people in that case it was bloggers and open source developers and there was something special
in the air we really felt like the case of wordpress it was decentralized publishing um that we thought
could be something really big and i remember at the time people told me especially investors that you
have a choice to make you can either build an open source community or you can build a company but you
can't do both and we thought actually we want to do both and we did one better wordpress continued to
thrive to this day 20 years later it's huge it now incredibly powers over 40 percent of all the
websites on the internet thriving as an open source community and we didn't just build one company we
helped build a whole ecosystem that is now also very big there's over 10 billion dollars a year that flows
through the wordpress ecosystem and automatic is less than 10 percent of that the rest is thousands tens
of thousands of other businesses people making a living on that platform so i've seen that happen i know
it's possible to build not just a company on an open ecosystem but lots of companies and um lots of
people being able to make a living and my hope is that i can bring some of that experience to this
community because i believe that the people are building the atmosphere people who are um coming up
